etymology.description: 肉(肉) (ròu, "meat") + 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") = meat used to feed dogs -> traditionally used as a phonetic component
etymology.interpretation: The character 肰 consists of 肉(肉) (ròu, "meat") and 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog"), combining the concepts of "meat" and "dog" to possibly convey the idea of meat being eaten by or associated with dogs. It primarily serves as a phonetic component in characters such as 然(然) (rán, "thus").
